text.title: AWS Cost Optimization Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every application of `AWS Cost Optimization` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Tag coverage MUST be ≥ 95% on (Project, Environment, Owner, ManagedBy) before a FinOps report is produced. Reports run on lower coverage are demoted to `partial`.

rationale.source: AWS Cost Allocation Tags docs; internal FinOps practice

rule.rationale: Cost reports on poorly-tagged accounts are noise; tag-coverage gate forces the foundational hygiene first.

rule.statement: Compute Savings Plan + RI coverage MUST target ≥ 70% for steady-state workloads. Coverage < 50% is flagged as a finding.

rationale.source: AWS Compute Savings Plans guide; FinOps Foundation benchmark

rule.rationale: Below 50% coverage leaves 30-60% of compute at On-Demand pricing — the easiest savings tier.

rule.statement: Graviton coverage MUST be ≥ 60% for new compute and ≥ 30% for existing workloads. Reports below are flagged as findings.
rule.rationale: Graviton is ~20% cheaper; missing it = paying x86 premium.

rule.statement: Fault-tolerant workloads (batch, dev, CI, async) MUST use Spot with diversified instance pools (≥ 4 types, ≥ 2 AZs). On-Demand for fault-tolerant workloads is flagged as a finding.
rule.rationale: Spot is 60-90% cheaper; missing Spot on batch is unjustifiable spend.

rule.statement: Non-prod environments (dev, staging, sandbox) MUST be stopped overnight + weekends via Instance Scheduler. 24/7 non-prod is flagged.
rule.rationale: Non-prod schedules save 60-75% on those resources; 24/7 non-prod is silent waste.

rule.statement: Rightsizing recommendations (Cost Explorer + Compute Optimizer) MUST be reviewed quarterly; underutilised resources (< 20% CPU, < 30% memory for 14d) MUST be downsized or terminated within 30 days of identification.
rule.rationale: Recommendations rot; quarterly cadence + 30d action SLA prevents accumulation.

rule.statement: AWS Cost Anomaly Detector MUST be enabled with a named alert owner + Slack / email destination. Anomalies without an owner are flagged.
rule.rationale: Cost spikes without an owner are detected post-bill; live detector + named owner catches them within 24h.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
